note: Contract-keyed deep capture (061-AT-SPEC) — the SIXTH and FINAL contract of the deep-capture program. One-time build-time fetch; CI never refetches (offline determinism) — ongoing freshness is the watcher's job via the registered plugin-marketplaces surface. Tier-2 conventions per 052-AT-SPEC: replace-in-place, lineage recorded (snapshot-updated in specs/lineage/log.jsonl), the extractor reads and never writes here. Like plugin-manifest (058-AT-SPEC), agent-definition (059-AT-SPEC), and hook-config (060-AT-SPEC): marketplace-catalog has NO machine-readable upstream schema and NO upstream spec versioning — the code.claude.com plugin-marketplaces reference page is the spec (the Marketplace-schema Required/Owner/Optional field tables, the Plugin-entries Required + two Optional tables, the Plugin-sources summary table + four per-type Field tables, and the Strict-mode table) and the marketplace.json catalog of anthropics/claude-plugins-official is real-world corroboration (authority precedence per 050-AT-SPEC: the reference page is the spec; samples corroborate). DEVIATION from 060: there is exactly ONE sample, but it is unusually strong — the official catalog IS the ground-truth marketplace (223 plugin entries at the pinned commit), so one sample carries more corroborating breadth than 058/059/060's 4-5 small files combined. At 126,953 bytes it sits under the 200KB truncation threshold, so the FULL file is vendored — no truncated projection-relevant slice was needed. NEGATIVE FACT: the reference page documents exactly one catalog file form (.claude-plugin/marketplace.json at the marketplace repo root); a direct URL serving marketplace.json is a distribution channel for the same form, not a second catalog form, so nothing further is vendored. spec_version is the capture date, not an upstream-declared revision. RE-VENDORED 2026-07-23 from the captured tier-2 snapshot specs/_vendor/plugin-marketplaces/snapshot.md (capture record archive/raw/plugin-marketplaces/20260723T001415Z.meta.json), gated on understanding all four findings rather than on them being absent. (1) catalog.renames - object, former-name to current-name migration map, Claude Code v2.1.193+. (2) plugin_entry.relevance - object, org-allowlisted plugin suggestion signals, v2.1.152+; it also joins the documented marketplace_specific_fields list. (3) catalog.reserved_names gains `first-party-plugins` and `healthcare`. Each has its own table row AND its own doc section on the captured page and is absent from the frozen one. (3) is the MATERIAL one and is not bookkeeping: the page now states Claude Code re-checks reserved names on EVERY marketplace load, not only on add, so a marketplace already registered under a newly-reserved name stops loading and reports an untrusted source (before v2.1.205 it kept loading). Estate exposure was checked, not assumed: no marketplace.json under the projects tree uses either new name; the IS marketplace is `claude-code-plugins-plus`, not reserved, though it IS a suffix-extension of the reserved `claude-code-plugins` and the same paragraph blocks impersonating names by prose judgement - a watch item, not a break. NO kernel authoring/v1 edit is implied: upstream-base/marketplace-catalog.v1.json is a required-set FLOOR that enumerates no optional plugin-entry fields and leaves additionalProperties permissive, so both new fields are already accepted and adding them would be cosmetic. Samples are deliberately NOT re-vendored.

files.version_basis: The page carries no version identifier and upstream publishes no machine-readable schema for the marketplace.json catalog (the $schema URL the official catalog cites, anthropic.com/claude-code/marketplace.schema.json, is an editor-autocomplete pointer the page says Claude Code ignores at load time — it is not a registry-registered surface); this .md form is the same URL/form the registry's fetch_plugin_marketplaces extractor monitors (capture.urls + ext .md in specs/upstream-surface-registry.v1.json).

files.selection_basis: THE official ground-truth catalog: Anthropic's own marketplace.json (owner = Anthropic), 223 plugin entries at the pinned commit — the same eb1510e1 pin the 058/059/060 samples use. Vendored WHOLE (126,953 bytes < the 200KB truncation threshold; no truncated slice needed). Structural breadth: 50 relative-path string sources (every one './'-prefixed), 173 object sources across github/url/git-subdir (npm documented but unobserved), 13 of the 20 documented plugin-entry fields exercised, strict:false in use, plus THREE upstream tolerances the page does not document — a commit key on 2 github sources, a path key on 5 url sources, and the plugin name wordpress.com (a dot, outside the documented kebab-case) — the capture's key observed-in-samples provenance findings.

samples_note: github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official is listed under unmonitored_candidates in specs/upstream-surface-registry.v1.json (not a registered surface; its registry row anticipates exactly this: 'Add the repo's marketplace manifest if a sampleable raw file is identified'); this vendor-meta entry is the sample's provenance record until a sampleable surface is registered per 050-AT-SPEC change discipline — the same arrangement the plugin-manifest (058), agent-definition (059), and hook-config (060) captures use. Ground-truth observations: all 5 top-level keys used ($schema, description, name, owner, plugins) are documented; owner carries the documented name + email; every plugin-entry field used is on a documented table; every object source carries the documented 'source' discriminator key and stays within the documented 4-type enum; all string sources honor the documented './' prefix rule. The three tolerances outside the documented surface (commit on github sources, path on url sources, the wordpress.com plugin name) are recorded observed-in-samples, never documented.
